key_insight: The paper turns the coupled Stokes-Biot fluid-poroelastic problem into a parallelizable interface problem by using Lagrange multipliers and a Schur complement reduction.
engineering_relevance: This enables scalable simulation of fluid–soft-structure interactions in applications such as biomechanics, geomechanics, and porous media flow coupling.
method: Decompose the domain into Stokes and Biot subdomains with Stokes-Stokes, Biot-Biot, and Stokes-Biot interfaces.
Enforce interface physics weakly using Lagrange multipliers for displacement, pressure, and normal stress/velocity continuity.
Solve the reduced multiplier system with GMRES, where each iteration requires independent local Stokes or Biot solves.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments and a field-of-value convergence analysis to assess the GMRES-based interface solver.
watch_item: The approach relies on stable mixed finite element spaces and careful multiplier/interface implementation, which can be sensitive in practice.
paper_title: Domain decomposition methods for the Stokes-Biot model of fluid-poroelastic structure interaction
